title: Worker → git-token-proxy (Unix socket)
description: Wire contract for the worker-side proxy hosted at `/run/generacy-git-token/control.sock` (override via env GIT_TOKEN_PROXY_SOCKET). The proxy exposes exactly one route — POST /git-token — and forwards it to the control-plane Unix socket at /run/generacy-control-plane/control.sock (override via env CONTROL_PLANE_SOCKET_PATH). Every other path/method returns 404 with an empty body and never contacts upstream. On success, the proxy passes through the upstream status and body verbatim (see contracts/control-plane-git-token.schema.json from #766 for that shape). On request body > 64 KiB → 413. On upstream transport failure (ECONNREFUSED, ENOENT, ECONNRESET, EPIPE, response timeout >= 30s) → 502 with code CONTROL_SOCKET_UNREACHABLE.

AllowedRequest.title: AllowedRequest
AllowedRequest.description: The only request shape forwarded upstream. Method MUST be POST and path MUST be exactly /git-token (trailing slash NOT allowed; query string allowed but ignored by the upstream).

path.description: Exact path match after URL parsing. /git-token/ and /git-tokens are rejected (404).

headers.description: Only Content-Type and Content-Length are forwarded upstream. All other headers (Host, Authorization, Accept, cookies, X-*, etc.) are stripped before the upstream HTTP request is issued.

content-length.description: Integer-as-string; the upstream value is recomputed by the proxy from the buffered body length, not copied from the inbound request.

body.description: Opaque to the proxy. Forwarded to upstream as raw bytes after size-cap enforcement. See contracts/control-plane-git-token.schema.json#/$defs/Request from #766 for the upstream-defined shape.

SuccessResponse.title: SuccessResponse
SuccessResponse.description: Passthrough of any 2xx response from upstream. Status code, headers, and body are forwarded unchanged. See contracts/control-plane-git-token.schema.json#/$defs/SuccessResponse from #766 for the upstream shape.

token.description: Opaque GitHub installation token. NEVER log this value. The proxy MUST NOT inspect or transform this field.

PayloadTooLargeResponse.title: PayloadTooLargeResponse
PayloadTooLargeResponse.description: HTTP 413 application/json. Triggered when the inbound request body exceeds 64 KiB. The proxy does NOT contact upstream in this case.

UpstreamUnreachableResponse.title: UpstreamUnreachableResponse
UpstreamUnreachableResponse.description: HTTP 502 application/json. Triggered when the upstream socket cannot be reached (ENOENT, ECONNREFUSED), the upstream connection is reset (ECONNRESET, EPIPE), or the upstream response timeout (30s) fires. Single error code for all transport-level failures; the per-request stdout log line distinguishes which one.

NotFoundResponse.title: NotFoundResponse
NotFoundResponse.description: HTTP 404 with empty body. Returned for ANY request the route allow-list rejects: wrong method (GET / PUT / DELETE / OPTIONS / etc. on /git-token), wrong path (anything other than exact /git-token), or trailing-slash variant (/git-token/). The proxy MUST NOT contact upstream in this case.

body.description: Empty body. No JSON, no error code — workers should treat 404 as 'this socket does not expose that route' and not retry.
